title: Why Sandboxing Is Critical
concept: Preventing agent-written code from deleting files, accessing system secrets, or spawning malicious processes.

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of a sandbox in coding?

options.text: To safely run code without affecting the system

exercises.explain: A sandbox helps keep the code separate from critical system areas.

cards.title: What Is Sandboxing?
cards.body: Sandboxing is a way to run code in a controlled environment. It isolates the code to prevent it from causing harm to the system.

cards.title: Why Use a Sandbox?
cards.body: Using a sandbox is essential to ensure that potentially harmful code cannot make unwanted changes, like deleting important files.

cards.title: Sandbox as a Castle
cards.body: Think of a sandbox like a castle with walls. While inside the walls, the code can play, but it can’t leave and damage anything outside.

cards.title: Example of Code in a Sandbox
cards.body: If you run a script that tries to delete a file, the sandbox will block that action, keeping your files safe.

cards.title: Myth: Sandboxes Limit Code Functionality
cards.body: Some think sandboxes restrict what code can do too much, but they actually help improve security and trust in running code.

cards.title: Sandboxing in Action
cards.body: Visualize a sandbox where you can play but can't leave to cause trouble.

exercises.prompt: What is one benefit of using a sandbox?

options.text: Code can run without damaging the system

exercises.explain: A sandbox allows code to run in a safe area, protecting the system from harmful actions.

exercises.prompt: Sandboxes prevent code from accessing critical system information.
exercises.explain: True! Sandboxes are designed to limit access to critical system information for safety.

exercises.prompt: A sandbox allows code to run in a __________ environment.
exercises.sentence: A sandbox allows code to run in a controlled environment.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'controlled' because it describes how a sandbox functions.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following statements correctly describes sandboxing?

options.text: Sandboxing allows any code to delete files freely.
Sandboxing keeps harmful code separated from the system.
Sandboxing gives code unlimited access to system resources.

exercises.explain: The correct answer is that sandboxing keeps harmful code separated from the system to ensure safety.

exercises.prompt: Why is it important to sandbox agent-written code?

options.text: To let them delete important files.
To let them safely execute operations without harm.
To allow access to system secrets easily.
To encourage agents to cause problems.

exercises.explain: The correct choice highlights that sandboxing allows for safe operations, preventing harm, and making our systems more secure.
